creator: brooks@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Grzegorz Junka from comment #0)

Anything that uses tools that manipulate LLVM IR (opt is one) needs to depend on a specific version of LLVM or it will break when LLVM is upgraded.  The idea that the "opt" binary in PATH will be the right one is not realistic.

Ideally, HIPE should configure it self using a provided llvm-config (e.g. /usr/local/bin/llvm-config36) and if it wants to use commands it could use `/usr/local/bin/llvm-config36 --bindir`/opt or use a wrapper script that puts bindir first in PATH.

There are usecases for devel/llvm and lang/clang metaports, but they would result in broken packages if abused for things like HIPE.
